differentials.name: Meconium ileus ◊  All newborns with meconium ileus have cystic fibrosis!
differentials.raw: Meconium ileus ◊  All newborns with meconium ileus have cystic fibrosis!

differentials.name: Colonic aganglionosis = Hirschsprüng disease
differentials.raw: Colonic aganglionosis = Hirschsprüng disease (may be associated with Down syndrome)

differentials.name: Anorectal atresia: associated with CNS abnormalities, part of VACTERL complex
differentials.raw: Anorectal atresia: associated with CNS abnormalities, part of VACTERL complex

differentials.name: Other cystic masses: duodenal atresia, hydronephrosis, ovarian cyst, mesenteric cyst
differentials.raw: Other cystic masses: duodenal atresia, hydronephrosis, ovarian cyst, mesenteric cyst

differentials.name: Normal small bowel variant
differentials.raw: Normal small bowel variant (especially < 20 weeks MA) with resolution on follow-up sonogram toward end of 2nd trimester (in 50-70%)

differentials.name: Normal colon variant in 3rd trimester ← echogenic meconium
differentials.raw: Normal colon variant in 3rd trimester ← echogenic meconium

differentials.name: Meconium ileus ◊  Increased abdominal echogenicity is seen in 60-70% of fetuses with cystic fibrosis!
differentials.raw: Meconium ileus ◊  Increased abdominal echogenicity is seen in 60-70% of fetuses with cystic fibrosis!

differentials.name: Chromosomal abnormality (3-25%) (a)  Down syndrome (5-14%) (b)  Trisomy 13, 18 (c)  Turner syndrome
differentials.raw: Chromosomal abnormality (3-25%) (a)  Down syndrome (5-14%) (b)  Trisomy 13, 18 (c)  Turner syndrome

differentials.name: Intraamniotic bleeding with subsequent swallowing of blood by fetus
differentials.raw: Intraamniotic bleeding with subsequent swallowing of blood by fetus

context: ◊  10-15% of newborns with cystic fibrosis present with meconium ileus! Bowel Obstruction in Fetus Etiology:   intestinal atresia / stenosis secondary to vascular accident, volvulus, meconium ileus, intussusception after organogenesis Prevalence:   imperforate anus 1÷3,000; small bowel 1÷5,000; colon 1÷20,000 Pathologic types: I one / more transverse diaphragms II blind-ending loops connected by fibrous string III complete separation of blind-ending loops IV apple-peel atresia of small bowel ← occlusion of SMA branch Associated with:   GI anomalies in 45% (malrotation, duplication, microcolon,
